item.title: Iwo Jima Veterans Reunite in Washington
item.description: Billy Byrd and Don Graves, both Iwo Jima veterans, reunite in Washington, DC, after serving as young Marines at Mount Suribachi in 1945.

media:content.media:title: Iwo Jima Veterans Reunite in Washington
media:content.media:description: Billy Byrd and Don Graves, both Iwo Jima veterans, reunite in Washington, DC, after serving as young Marines at Mount Suribachi in 1945.

item.title: One Man, an Army: Medal of Honor Charge
item.description: A Medal of Honor story of courage and leadership, told through one man’s daring charge and the historical context behind his service.

media:content.media:title: One Man, an Army: Medal of Honor Charge
media:content.media:description: A Medal of Honor story of courage and leadership, told through one man’s daring charge and the historical context behind his service.

item.title: Bravery Witnessed on 9/11
item.description: A firsthand reflection on the bravery witnessed on September 11, honoring courage, service, and the people who responded that day.

media:content.media:title: Bravery Witnessed on 9/11
media:content.media:description: A firsthand reflection on the bravery witnessed on September 11, honoring courage, service, and the people who responded that day.

item.title: Not Getting a Morphine Shot Saved My Life
item.description: A firsthand war story from the American Veterans Center about how one decision in the field helped save a life.

media:content.media:title: Not Getting a Morphine Shot Saved My Life
media:content.media:description: A firsthand war story from the American Veterans Center about how one decision in the field helped save a life.

item.title: The Biggest Problem With Bazookas in WWII
item.description: A historical look at the main challenge soldiers faced with bazookas during World War II, based on veteran context and wartime experience.

media:content.media:title: The Biggest Problem With Bazookas in WWII
media:content.media:description: A historical look at the main challenge soldiers faced with bazookas during World War II, based on veteran context and wartime experience.

item.title: How a Beer Run Became a WWII Legend
item.description: A wartime story from World War II, told through the American Veterans Center. A simple errand became part of a lasting military legend.

media:content.media:title: How a Beer Run Became a WWII Legend
media:content.media:description: A wartime story from World War II, told through the American Veterans Center. A simple errand became part of a lasting military legend.

item.title: Veterans Recall Combat in Afghanistan
item.description: A veteran reflects on combat in Afghanistan and the realities of military service, offering a firsthand account of war, duty, and survival.

media:content.media:title: Veterans Recall Combat in Afghanistan
media:content.media:description: A veteran reflects on combat in Afghanistan and the realities of military service, offering a firsthand account of war, duty, and survival.

item.title: General Patton's Personal Jeep Driver Remembers the Legendary Man
item.description:
Francis "Jeep" Sanza was drafted into the U.S. Army in the spring of 1941. Due to his mechanical prowess the army put him into the 3457th Ordnance Medium Auto Maintenance Company, where he began driving the tough new Willys MB 1/4-ton reconnaissance vehicles, later to be simply called the "Jeep".
Sanza was eventually chosen to be the personal driver and mechanic for General George S. Patton, one of the most important and revered generals of the Second World War.
Sanza's unique position gave him a window into the mind of one of the greatest strategists in modern military history, as he drove Patton across France and into Germany.
Francis "Jeep" Sanza passed away peacefully on January 30, 2018

media:content.media:title: General Patton's Personal Jeep Driver Remembers the Legendary Man
media:content.media:description:
Francis "Jeep" Sanza was drafted into the U.S. Army in the spring of 1941. Due to his mechanical prowess the army put him into the 3457th Ordnance Medium Auto Maintenance Company, where he began driving the tough new Willys MB 1/4-ton reconnaissance vehicles, later to be simply called the "Jeep".
Sanza was eventually chosen to be the personal driver and mechanic for General George S. Patton, one of the most important and revered generals of the Second World War.
Sanza's unique position gave him a window into the mind of one of the greatest strategists in modern military history, as he drove Patton across France and into Germany.
Francis "Jeep" Sanza passed away peacefully on January 30, 2018

item.description: A Memorial Day tribute honors the service members who gave their lives for the nation, recognizing sacrifice, remembrance, and national gratitude.

item.title: A Jewish PFC Interrogates a German General
item.description: A firsthand wartime account from a Jewish PFC who questioned a German general, offering a personal view of service, duty, and history.
